name: Eclipse JDT Language Server
description: Java language server providing code completion, go-to-definition, refactoring, diagnostics, and project-aware intelligence for Maven and Gradle projects
when_to_use: Always active for Java projects — provides IDE intelligence including type inference, imports, and refactoring

install.command: brew install jdtls || download from https://download.eclipse.org/jdtls/snapshots/

name: mvn compile
description: Compile a Java Maven project and resolve all dependencies from pom.xml
when_to_use: To compile the project, resolve dependencies, or check for compilation errors in Maven-based projects

install.command: brew install maven || sdk install maven

name: gradle build
description: Compile and assemble a Java Gradle project including all dependencies from build.gradle
when_to_use: To compile, assemble, and package Gradle-based Java projects

install.command: brew install gradle || sdk install gradle

name: mvn test
description: Run unit tests in a Maven project using Surefire plugin — executes JUnit and TestNG tests
when_to_use: After code changes, before commits, in CI pipelines for Maven projects
what_it_finds: Test failures, assertion errors, uncaught exceptions, coverage gaps

install.command: brew install maven || sdk install maven

name: gradle test
description: Run unit tests in a Gradle project — executes JUnit and TestNG tests with parallel support
when_to_use: After code changes, before commits, in CI pipelines for Gradle projects
what_it_finds: Test failures, assertion errors, uncaught exceptions

install.command: brew install gradle || sdk install gradle

name: JUnit Console Runner
description: Standalone JUnit 5 test runner — execute tests directly without Maven or Gradle
when_to_use: Run individual test classes or methods directly, useful for quick iteration and debugging tests
what_it_finds: Test failures, assertion errors, test discovery issues

install.command: download junit-platform-console-standalone.jar from Maven Central

name: Checkstyle
description: Java static analysis tool enforcing coding standards — checks naming conventions, Javadoc, whitespace, imports, and design patterns
when_to_use: Before commits, in CI — enforce consistent code style and catch style violations
what_it_finds: Naming violations, missing Javadoc, import order issues, line length violations, coding standard breaches

install.command: brew install checkstyle || download from https://checkstyle.org

name: SpotBugs
description: Static analysis tool that finds bug patterns in Java bytecode — detects null pointer dereferences, infinite loops, resource leaks, and concurrency issues
when_to_use: After compilation, in CI — catches real bugs that compile but are likely wrong
what_it_finds: Null pointer dereferences, resource leaks, thread safety issues, performance anti-patterns, dead code

install.command: brew install spotbugs || download from https://spotbugs.github.io

name: PMD
description: Source code analyzer finding common programming flaws — unused variables, empty catch blocks, unnecessary object creation, and copy-paste detection
when_to_use: Before commits, in CI — finds code smells and potential bugs at source level
what_it_finds: Unused variables, empty catch blocks, dead code, copy-paste issues (CPD), overcomplicated expressions

install.command: brew install pmd || download from https://pmd.github.io

name: OWASP Dependency-Check
description: Scan Java project dependencies for known CVEs using the National Vulnerability Database — identifies vulnerable libraries in Maven and Gradle projects
when_to_use: Before releases, in CI, after dependency updates — critical for security compliance
what_it_finds: CVEs in dependencies, outdated vulnerable libraries, transitive dependency risks

install.command: brew install dependency-check || download from https://owasp.org/www-project-dependency-check/

name: Spotless
description: Code formatter for Java supporting google-java-format, eclipse, and palantir styles — integrates with Maven and Gradle
when_to_use: Before every commit to ensure consistent code formatting across the project

install.command: Add com.diffplug.spotless plugin to build.gradle or pom.xml

name: mvn release
description: Automate Maven project releases — bump version, create git tag, deploy artifacts to repository
when_to_use: When publishing releases, cutting new versions of Maven artifacts

install.command: brew install maven || sdk install maven

name: Gradle Wrapper
description: Generate or update the Gradle wrapper — ensures all developers and CI use the same Gradle version without a local install
when_to_use: When setting up a new Gradle project or upgrading the Gradle version

install.command: brew install gradle || sdk install gradle

name: jdb
description: Java command-line debugger — attach to running JVM processes, set breakpoints, inspect variables, and step through code
when_to_use: Debugging Java applications, inspecting runtime state, diagnosing crashes or unexpected behavior

install.command: Included with JDK

name: Java Flight Recorder
description: Low-overhead JVM profiler built into the JDK — records CPU usage, memory allocation, garbage collection, thread contention, and I/O events
when_to_use: Performance profiling, diagnosing slow methods, memory leaks, GC pressure, or thread contention
what_it_finds: Hot methods, memory allocation sites, GC pauses, lock contention, I/O bottlenecks

install.command: Included with JDK 11+

name: async-profiler
description: Low-overhead sampling profiler for JVM — generates CPU and allocation flame graphs without safepoint bias
when_to_use: Performance analysis requiring accurate flame graphs, profiling CPU hotspots or memory allocation patterns
what_it_finds: CPU hotspots, allocation-heavy code paths, lock contention, native method overhead

install.command: download from https://github.com/async-profiler/async-profiler/releases

name: Apache Maven
description: Declarative build and project management tool for Java — manages dependencies, builds, reporting, and documentation via pom.xml with a standardized project lifecycle
when_to_use: As the primary build tool for Maven-based Java projects — handles dependency resolution, compilation, packaging, and deployment in a standardized way

install.command: brew install maven || sdk install maven || apt install maven

name: Bazel
description: Google's polyglot build system — hermetic, reproducible builds with aggressive caching and remote execution support for large-scale Java monorepos
when_to_use: For large Java monorepos needing reproducible builds, remote caching, and fine-grained dependency tracking across thousands of targets

install.command: brew install bazel || download from https://bazel.build/install

name: google-java-format
description: Opinionated Java code formatter that follows Google Java Style Guide — reformats entire files with no configuration, ensuring consistent style across teams
when_to_use: Before every commit when your project uses Google Java Style — also usable as a Spotless backend or IntelliJ plugin

install.command: brew install google-java-format || download jar from https://github.com/google/google-java-format/releases

name: JUnit 5
description: The standard testing framework for Java — JUnit 5 (Jupiter) provides annotations, assertions, parameterized tests, nested tests, extensions, and test lifecycle management
when_to_use: For all Java unit and integration testing — the de facto standard, supported by every IDE, build tool, and CI platform

install.command: Add org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter dependency to Maven or Gradle

name: TestNG
description: Powerful Java testing framework inspired by JUnit and NUnit — supports data-driven testing, parallel execution, test dependencies, and flexible test configuration via XML
when_to_use: For complex test suites needing test dependencies, data providers, parallel execution groups, or XML-based suite configuration

install.command: Add org.testng:testng dependency to Maven or Gradle

name: Mockito
description: The most popular Java mocking framework — creates mock objects, stubs method behavior, and verifies interactions with a clean, fluent API
when_to_use: When unit testing code that depends on collaborators and you need to isolate the class under test with mock dependencies

install.command: Add org.mockito:mockito-core dependency to Maven or Gradle

name: AssertJ
description: Fluent assertion library for Java — provides rich, type-safe assertions for collections, strings, exceptions, dates, and custom types with IDE-friendly chaining
when_to_use: When JUnit assertions feel too verbose — AssertJ makes test assertions more readable and provides better error messages

install.command: Add org.assertj:assertj-core dependency to Maven or Gradle

name: JaCoCo
description: Java code coverage library — instruments bytecode to measure line, branch, and instruction coverage, generating HTML and XML reports for CI integration
when_to_use: In CI pipelines to enforce coverage thresholds, and locally to identify untested code paths
what_it_finds: Untested code paths, uncovered branches, missed lines, coverage regressions

install.command: Add org.jacoco:jacoco-maven-plugin to pom.xml or jacoco Gradle plugin

name: jlink
description: Java linker that creates custom, minimal JRE images containing only the modules your application needs — reduces deployment size from hundreds of MBs to tens of MBs
when_to_use: When creating minimal Docker images or distributable Java applications with a stripped-down runtime

install.command: Included with JDK 9+

name: jpackage
description: Java packaging tool that creates platform-specific installers (MSI, DMG, DEB, RPM) for Java applications — bundles a custom JRE with the application
when_to_use: When distributing Java desktop applications to end users who may not have Java installed

install.command: Included with JDK 14+

name: javadoc
description: Official Java documentation generator — produces HTML API reference docs from Javadoc comments in source code with cross-references, search, and module support
when_to_use: When generating API documentation for Java libraries or projects, before releases or for internal docs

install.command: Included with JDK

name: Spring Boot CLI
description: Command-line interface for Spring Boot — quickly scaffold new projects, run Groovy scripts, and manage Spring Boot applications from the terminal
when_to_use: When bootstrapping new Spring Boot projects or running quick prototypes with Groovy-based Spring scripts

install.command: brew install spring-boot || sdk install springboot

name: Quarkus CLI
description: Command-line tool for Quarkus framework — scaffold projects, add extensions, run dev mode with live reload, and build native executables via GraalVM
when_to_use: When building cloud-native Java applications with Quarkus — provides fast startup, low memory footprint, and native compilation

install.command: brew install quarkusio/tap/quarkus || sdk install quarkus

name: Micronaut CLI
description: Command-line tool for Micronaut framework — scaffold projects, generate controllers/services, and build lightweight cloud-native Java applications with compile-time DI
when_to_use: When building microservices and serverless functions with Micronaut — compile-time dependency injection for fast startup and low memory

install.command: brew install --cask micronaut-projects/tap/micronaut || sdk install micronaut

name: Jib
description: Google's containerization tool for Java — builds optimized Docker images without a Dockerfile or Docker daemon, with layer caching for fast rebuilds
when_to_use: When containerizing Java applications for Kubernetes or cloud deployment without writing Dockerfiles or installing Docker locally

install.command: Add com.google.cloud.tools.jib-maven-plugin to pom.xml or jib-gradle-plugin to build.gradle

name: Snyk for Java
description: Developer-first security tool — scans Java Maven and Gradle projects for known vulnerabilities in dependencies, license issues, and code security problems
when_to_use: In CI pipelines and during development to find and fix security vulnerabilities in Java dependencies before they reach production
what_it_finds: CVEs in dependencies, license compliance issues, vulnerable transitive dependencies, fixable upgrade paths

install.command: brew install snyk || npm install -g snyk

name: PIT Mutation Testing
description: Mutation testing tool for Java — modifies your code (inserts bugs) and checks if tests catch them, measuring the real effectiveness of your test suite beyond coverage
when_to_use: When you need to evaluate test suite quality beyond line coverage — mutation testing reveals tests that pass but do not actually verify behavior
what_it_finds: Weak tests that pass despite code mutations, insufficient assertions, tests that verify nothing meaningful

install.command: Add org.pitest:pitest-maven to pom.xml or info.solidsoft.pitest Gradle plugin

name: ArchUnit
description: Architecture testing library for Java — write unit tests that enforce architectural rules like layer dependencies, naming conventions, and package structure
when_to_use: When you need to enforce architectural constraints in code — prevent dependency cycles, enforce package boundaries, validate naming conventions
what_it_finds: Architecture violations, circular dependencies, package boundary breaches, naming convention violations, layer access violations

install.command: Add com.tngtech.archunit:archunit-junit5 dependency to Maven or Gradle

name: JMH
description: Java Microbenchmark Harness by OpenJDK — the standard framework for writing reliable JVM benchmarks with proper warmup, JIT handling, and statistical analysis
when_to_use: When measuring the performance of Java code with statistical rigor — handles JVM warmup, dead code elimination, and constant folding that invalidate naive benchmarks
what_it_finds: Performance regressions, throughput characteristics, latency distributions, allocation rates

install.command: Add org.openjdk.jmh:jmh-core and jmh-generator-annprocess dependencies

name: Error Prone
description: Google's compile-time static analysis tool for Java — hooks into javac to catch common bugs at compile time with auto-fix suggestions
when_to_use: As a javac plugin in your build to catch bugs at compile time — more precise than post-hoc linters because it uses the compiler's type information
what_it_finds: Null dereferences, incorrect equals/hashCode, resource leaks, format string errors, thread safety issues, common API misuse

install.command: Add com.google.errorprone:error_prone_core to annotationProcessorPaths in Maven or Gradle

name: GraalVM Native Image
description: Ahead-of-time compiler that produces standalone native executables from Java applications — eliminates JVM startup time and reduces memory footprint
when_to_use: When building serverless functions, CLI tools, or microservices that need instant startup and minimal memory — works with Quarkus, Micronaut, and Spring Boot

install.command: sdk install java 22.r21-grl || brew install --cask graalvm-jdk

name: jEnv
description: Java version manager — switch between multiple JDK versions per-project or globally, similar to rbenv for Ruby or pyenv for Python
when_to_use: When working on multiple Java projects requiring different JDK versions on the same machine

install.command: brew install jenv || git clone https://github.com/jenv/jenv.git ~/.jenv

name: SDKMAN!
description: Software development kit manager for JVM languages — install, manage, and switch between multiple versions of Java, Gradle, Maven, Kotlin, Scala, and more
when_to_use: When setting up JVM development environments — manages JDK versions and related tools with simple install/use/default commands

install.command: curl -s https://get.sdkman.io | bash

name: Project Lombok
description: Annotation processor that reduces Java boilerplate — generates getters, setters, builders, equals/hashCode, toString, and logging fields at compile time
when_to_use: When Java boilerplate is overwhelming — Lombok annotations like @Data, @Builder, @Slf4j eliminate repetitive code while keeping classes clean

install.command: Add org.projectlombok:lombok dependency and annotationProcessor to Maven or Gradle
